sections.content: I see the first green block (top-left) changed from green (N) to sky blue (S)! Looking at the image, the leftmost green block at the top is now sky blue. This is progress! The MOUSE action on the green block changed its color. Let me check the state more carefully and continue clicking the remaining green blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Count green blocks now
green_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Green blocks remaining: {len(green_nodes)}")
for n in green_nodes:
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")

# Check sky blue regions - one should have grown
blue_regions = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'S']
for br in blue_regions:
    print(f"Sky blue {br['id']}: pixels={br['pixels']}")

# Check purple (HUD progress)
purple_node = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'p']
if purple_node:
    print(f"Purple (HUD): pixels={purple_node[0]['pixels']}")

# Check result
print(f"\nResult: score={last_action_result.get('score')}, gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
